trivia_bullets.text: The Mire of Punishment is a specific geographic region within Theros's Underworld, reserved for souls who attempted to escape and were caught. Flavor text: 'Those caught attempting to escape the Underworld spend the rest of their existence trapped in the Mire of Punishment.' The Underworld's geography also includes the Labyrinth of Memories, the Nerono ocean, and Ilysia (haven for fallen heroes).
sources: WebSearch snippet: DiceTry.com / MTG Wiki: Underworld

trivia_bullets.text: Mire's Grasp is a {1}{B} Enchantment Aura that gives -3/-3 — a functional reprint of Dead Weight (Innistrad, 2011) in enchantment-aura form. Hipsters of the Coast rated it A: 'Dead Weight is awesome. Mire's Grasp is deader weight and also awesome,' noting it efficiently kills most common creatures while supporting the WB auras archetype.
sources: Hipsters of the Coast: Every Common in Theros Beyond Death Reviewed

trivia_bullets.text: Printing Mire's Grasp as an Enchantment (rather than an instant as Dead Weight was) was a deliberate design choice for THB: the set's Constellation mechanic rewards playing enchantments, so even removal spells were templated as enchantments to double as constellation triggers and aura-synergy pieces.
sources: WebSearch snippet: Star City Games: Enchantments in Theros Beyond Death Limited

related_cards.name: Dead Weight
related_cards.note: Direct mechanical predecessor; Mire's Grasp is the enchantment-frame iteration of the same -3/-3 design

related_cards.name: Mire Triton
related_cards.note: Shares the Mire name and THB underworld setting

related_cards.name: Underworld Dreams
related_cards.note: Same underworld thematic space in THB's black suite
